learning_outcomes.description: Be conversant with the fundamental requirements of the curating vocation in the visual arts.
Understand the processes from conception to delivery of a visual arts exhibition.
Conduct scholarly, well-focused research for visual arts curating, with the ability to communicate curatorial ideas in written forms, including in exhibition rationales and other exhibition texts.
Think critically about exhibitions and be able to engage in oral and written discourse and debate relevant to the field.
Be conversant with key theoretical discourses, histories and practices in the field of curating.

assessments.task_description:
Select ONE art work from the suite of works provided on Blackboard and write a 700 word acquisition rationale explaining why this work should be purchased for the University of Queensland Art Collection.

Marking criteria:

  • Demonstrated ability to locate pertinent biographical details in order to make a summary statement about the artist’s significance;
  • Incorporation of visual analysis of an artwork, with specific emphasis on its relationship to the artist’s broader practice;
  • Demonstrated ability to locate the artwork in the context of the UQ Art Collection;
  • Ability to write in a format suited to an acquisition rationale, using clear and fluent written expression suitable for an educated readership.


Format: Your acquisition rationale must have three components (a) overview of the artist’s biography, (b) a rationale explaining the significance of the artwork proposed for acquisition, and (c) a strong case for why the artwork fits within the broader context of the UQ Art Collection. The order of the components can be tailored as required.

The word length is 700 words. Writing to a specific word length is a highly desirable skill in the workplace and students should pay particular attention to organising their material to accommodate this requirement. Footnotes are not included in word length. There is no requirement for a separate bibliography.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please submit through TurnItIn as a Word or PDF doc only.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: Write an 800 word critical review of a current or recent exhibition of visual art held at one of the following galleries: Milani Gallery, Ryan Renshaw gallery, UQ Art Museum, Griffith University Art Museum, Queensland Art Gallery / Gallery of Modern Art, HOTA Surfer's Paradise, QUT Art Gallery. If you find an exhibition that is NOT at one of these galleries that you wish to write about, please check with the Course Coordinator first.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: Submit a proposal for a collection-based exhibition that would be suitable for display in one of the rooms in Griffith University Museum of Art. You must select only works from the Griffith University art collection to go in your show. Your proposal should incude: (i) a 1000 word exhibition rationale describing the exhibition concept and objectives (ii) a list of works with full media including images (iii) a mock up floor plan indicating potential layout in the space (iii) artist bios for all participating artists and (iv) bullet points 3-4 ideas for possible public engagement programs that might accompany the exhibition. All works must be sourced from the Griffith University Museum of Art collection only. Artists' bios, list of works and floor plan are not included in the word count.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via TurnItIn through Blackboard as Word doc of PDF only
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 1: Introduction to the course

What is a curator? What is an exhibition?
During this introductory week we explore the histories of these concepts, relating them to practices of collecting, caring for, and exhibiting works of art.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 1: Introduction to the course What is a curator? What is an exhibition? During this introductory week we explore the histories of these concepts, relating them to practices of collecting, caring for, and exhibiting works of art.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 2: Behind the Scenes with UQ Art Museum staff

Hear from UQ Art Museum staff about their experiences of working in the gallery and their career trajectories in the industry. Q&A with museum staff facilitated by Dr Dominique Chen.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_title: Week 2: Behind the Scenes with UQ Art Museum staff
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 2: Behind the Scenes with UQ Art Museum staff Hear from UQ Art Museum staff about their experiences of working in the gallery and their career trajectories in the industry. Q&A with museum staff facilitated by Dr Dominique Chen.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 3: Writing Workshop: Exhibition Review and Acquisition Rationale

Writing for curators: this week focuses on skills needed to write an exhibition review and an acquisition rationale. Please bring your laptop to class for the writing workshop.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Week 3: Writing Workshop: Exhibition Review and Acquisition Rationale
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 3: Writing Workshop: Exhibition Review and Acquisition Rationale Writing for curators: this week focuses on skills needed to write an exhibition review and an acquisition rationale. Please bring your laptop to class for the writing workshop.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 4: Site visit Griffith University Art Museum

Site visit to Griffith University Art Museum, Southbank, in preparation for your exhibition proposal assessment. Hear from GUAM staff members about their professional trajectories, learn about the specifics of a University-based art museum collection and begin considering what you might like to include in your curated exhibition proposal.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Week 4: Site visit Griffith University Art Museum
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 4: Site visit Griffith University Art Museum Site visit to Griffith University Art Museum, Southbank, in preparation for your exhibition proposal assessment. Hear from GUAM staff members about their professional trajectories, learn about the specifics of a University-based art museum collection and begin considering what you might like to include in your curated exhibition proposal.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 5: Site Visit Institute of Modern Art and Outer Space

Site visit to Institute of Modern Art, Fortitude Valley, and Outer Space. Curatorial walkthrough of exhibition. Learn about what it is like to work in a non-collecting, contemporary arts institution. Meet in the foyer of the IMA.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Week 5: Site Visit Institute of Modern Art and Outer Space
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 5: Site Visit Institute of Modern Art and Outer Space Site visit to Institute of Modern Art, Fortitude Valley, and Outer Space. Curatorial walkthrough of exhibition. Learn about what it is like to work in a non-collecting, contemporary arts institution. Meet in the foyer of the IMA.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 6: Site visit QAGOMA

Curatorial walkthrough of select exhibitions with QAGOMA curatorial staff and curatorial managers. Learn about state-funded arts institutions and discover how exhibitions are developed and presented to the public. Meet in the foyer of Gallery of Modern Art (ground floor) at the bag check.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 6: Site visit QAGOMA Curatorial walkthrough of select exhibitions with QAGOMA curatorial staff and curatorial managers. Learn about state-funded arts institutions and discover how exhibitions are developed and presented to the public. Meet in the foyer of Gallery of Modern Art (ground floor) at the bag check.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 7: Indigenous Perspectives in Curating

Guest lecture with Quandamooka curator Freja Carmichael, in conversation with Gamilaroi scholar Dr Dominique Chen. Considers specific principles of curating in collaboration with community, relational objectives, and negotiations between Indigenous and non-Indigenous stakeholders.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 7: Indigenous Perspectives in Curating Guest lecture with Quandamooka curator Freja Carmichael, in conversation with Gamilaroi scholar Dr Dominique Chen. Considers specific principles of curating in collaboration with community, relational objectives, and negotiations between Indigenous and non-Indigenous stakeholders.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 8: Writing Workshop Developing an Exhibition Proposal

Seminar on developing a collections-based exhibition proposal, in preparation for your final assignment. Please bring your laptop to class for the writing workshop.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Week 8: Writing Workshop Developing an Exhibition Proposal
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 8: Writing Workshop Developing an Exhibition Proposal Seminar on developing a collections-based exhibition proposal, in preparation for your final assignment. Please bring your laptop to class for the writing workshop.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 9: Site Visit Milani Gallery

Site visit to Milani Gallery in West End, for a visit to the exhibitions and discussion with gallery staff about the workings of the commercial art market.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 9: Site Visit Milani Gallery Site visit to Milani Gallery in West End, for a visit to the exhibitions and discussion with gallery staff about the workings of the commercial art market.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 10: Exhibition Texts and Audiences

Introduction to the diverse range of texts produced around exhibitions, including didactics and curatorial statements. Seminar also includes final discussion of Exhibition Proposal assessment with opportunity for questions.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 10: Exhibition Texts and Audiences Introduction to the diverse range of texts produced around exhibitions, including didactics and curatorial statements. Seminar also includes final discussion of Exhibition Proposal assessment with opportunity for questions.
